document.title: IC Test Report
standards: FCC KDB 558074 D01 DTS Meas Guidance v04

sourceSummary.oneLine: IC test report CR740701AE Rev. 02 for Laird Technologies ST60-2230C Bluetooth LE operation under RSS-247 and related test standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory evidence that the tested representative sample was evaluated for IC/RSS transmitter requirements and reported as passing the listed test items.
keyPoints: Equipment is identified as an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module, model ST60-2230C.
Report Rev. 02 was issued Jul. 21, 2017 with description 'Revised model name'.
The representative tested model was ST60-2230C; SU60-2230C is also listed among models provided to the EUT.
Bluetooth LE operation is listed for 2402-2480 MHz, channels 0-39, 1 Mbps, using GFSK modulation.
Summary of Test Results reports Pass for AC power line conducted emissions, radiated emissions, maximum output power, 6 dB bandwidth, power spectral density, and antenna requirement.

entities.name: Laird Technologies
entities.evidence: Brand Name : Laird Technologies Applicant : Laird Technologies

entities.name: International Certification Corp.
entities.evidence: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

entities.name: SU60-2230C
entities.evidence: The following models are provided to this EUT. Brand Name Model Name ... Laird Technologies ST60-2230C ... SU60-2230C

concepts.name: Bluetooth LE
concepts.evidence: Bluetooth Mode Ch. Freq. (MHz) Channel Number Data Rate 2400-2483.5 V4.2 LE 2402-2480 0-39 [40] 1 Mbps

concepts.name: 6 dB bandwidth
concepts.evidence: The minimum 6dB bandwidth shall be at least 500 kHz.

concepts.name: Power spectral density
concepts.evidence: Power spectral density shall not be greater than 8 dBm in any 3 kHz band.

evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: CR740701AE Page : 1 of 40 Report Version: Rev. 02

evidence.claim: Rev. 02 was issued on Jul. 21, 2017 for revised model name.
evidence.evidenceText: CR740701AE Rev. 02 Revised model name Jul. 21, 2017

evidence.claim: The equipment is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
ST60-2230C was selected as the representative final test model.
evidence.evidenceText: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

evidence.claim: The tested Bluetooth LE frequency range is 2402-2480 MHz with 40 channels at 1 Mbps.
Bluetooth LE uses GFSK modulation in this report.
evidence.evidenceText: Note 1: Bluetooth LE (Low energy) uses GFSK modulation.

evidence.claim: The EUT power supply type is DC 3.3V from host.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Type DC 3.3V from host

evidence.claim: The report lists RSS-247, RSS-Gen, ANSI C63.10, ANSI C63.4, and FCC KDB 558074 D01 as applicable standards or documents.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the specification of EUT, the EUT must comply with following standards and KDB documents. RSS-247 Issue 2 February 2017 RSS-Gen Issue 4 November 2014 ANSI C63.10-2013 ANSI C63.4-2014 FCC KDB 558074 D01 DTS Meas Guidance v04

evidence.claim: Summary test results report Pass for conducted emissions, radiated emissions, maximum output power, 6 dB bandwidth, power spectral density, and antenna requirement.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of Test Results ... AC Power Line Conducted Emissions ... Pass ... Radiated Emissions ... Pass ... Maximum Output Power ... Pass ... 6dB Bandwidth ... Pass ... Power Spectral Density ... Pass ... Antenna Requirement ... Pass

evidence.claim: Maximum output power measured for BT LE includes 10.80 dBm at 2402 MHz.
The reported 6 dB bandwidth is 0.640 MHz at 2402, 2440, and 2480 MHz against a 500 kHz limit.
Power spectral density results are below the 8 dBm/3 kHz limit.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is identified in the source as IC/RSS rules and an IC identifier, but the document is a laboratory test report rather than a regulatory grant or certificate.
Some plot pages contain captions and setup references without readable numeric plot data in the extracted text.
